title: Sunrun Increases Storage Attachment Rate to 62%
text: Sunrun achieved 62% energy storage attachment rate in Q4 2024, up from 60% in Q3, indicating percentage of customers opting for battery storage when installing solar panels. Attachment rates differ significantly by geography with Hawaii and Puerto Rico leading at nearly 100%, California at approximately 89%, and Texas at 66%, with remaining US regions lagging at just 10%. Storage metrics show 392MWh added in Q4 2024, up 78% from same period in 2023, while solar installations grew more modestly at 6% year-on-year. By year-end 2024, Sunrun's cumulative capacity reached 2.5GWh of storage and 7.5GW of installed solar. The company generated positive cash flow of $34 million in Q4 2024, marking third consecutive profitable quarter. Moving forward, Sunrun prioritizes cash generation and targets higher-value products, aiming to increase storage attachment rates to 66% throughout 2025. Full-year 2024 storage attachment rate was 57%. The company's strategy focuses on leveraging storage systems for grid support programs and virtual power plant participation, creating additional revenue streams while supporting grid reliability.
